design_philosophy.core_loop: Complete aviation missions across multiple vehicle types (light plane, hang glider, skydiving, rocket belt) to earn pilot licenses and progress through difficulty tiers
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Mastering contextual flight physics and control schemes unique to each vehicle while navigating environmental constraints and landing zones
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Learning to pilot various aircraft and experiencing the freedom of flight across detailed environments; demonstrating mastery through successive license achievements
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Pilotwings is fundamentally pedagogical—it functions as a flight simulator tutorial wrapped in game progression. Each vehicle type has distinct, non-transferable controls, forcing players to learn new mechanics repeatedly rather than building on prior mastery. This design prioritizes variety and realism over mechanical depth, treating flight simulation fidelity as the core experience rather than action gameplay. The game confidently states: 'flight is about context-specific skill, not universal mastery.'

difficulty.continues_system: Mission-based checkpoint system; players can retry failed missions without penalty
difficulty.checkpointing: Each mission/event is a discrete checkpoint; failure in one mission does not reset progress in previously completed events
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players progress through licenses in structured sequence with increasing challenge; bonus stages and attack helicopter modes provide optional harder content; no explicit difficulty select documented

progression.upgrade_system: Pilot license tiers (bronze, silver, gold implied by 'lessons' structure); each license unlock grants access to new vehicle types and more challenging missions
progression.unlock_mechanism: Completing missions with sufficient skill (implicit scoring/grading) unlocks next license tier and additional vehicles (light plane → hang gliding → skydiving → rocket belt)
replayability_hooks: Bonus stages separate from main progression
Multiple vehicle types encourage re-engagement with different control schemes
Implicit score/rating system suggests score chasing on completed missions

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling through detailed flight environments
Licensing framework provides loose narrative scaffolding (earning pilot credentials)
Vehicle type variety implies progression from basic to advanced aviation

environmental_storytelling: Detailed 3D environments (using Mode 7 scaling) showcase landscapes and flight geography
Different vehicle contexts (civilian flight, emergency skydiving, military helicopter) imply varied aviation scenarios
Landing zones and environmental obstacles provide implicit mission parameters
